\chapter{Boolean Analysis — Recursive Reduction}
%%% generated by the blueprint pipeline from TCSlib.BooleanAnalysis.LMN.RecursiveReduction
\section{Overview}

This module collects the helper lemmas used in the recursive proof of the LMN circuit
reduction. It relates restrictions, the structural parameters of a circuit (depth, size,
fan-in) to those of its children, expresses the evaluation of an AND/OR node under a
restriction, and combines compression with the switching lemma to bound the decision-tree
depth of a node after a further random restriction.

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
\section{Declarations}

% LMN.restrictFn_composeRestr is documented in Depth3Switching.tex (same private
% lemma name appears in this module too; the binding label lives there to stay unique).

\begin{lemma}[Child depth bound]
\lean{LMN.children_depth_le}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.depth}
If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has depth at most $d$, then every child
$c \in cs$ has depth at most $d - 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Sum of child sizes bound]
\lean{LMN.children_size_sum_le}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size}
If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has size at most $s$, then the sum of
the sizes of its children is at most $s - 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Child fan-in bound]
\lean{LMN.children_maxFanin_le}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.maxFanin}
If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$, then
every child $c \in cs$ has maximum fan-in at most $w$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Child size bounded by parent]
\lean{LMN.child_size_le_parent}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.size, LMN.children_size_sum_le}
If a node circuit $\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs$ has size at most $s$, then every
child $c \in cs$ has size at most $s - 1$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Restricted evaluation of a node]
\lean{LMN.restrictFn_node_eval}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn}
The restriction of the evaluation of a node circuit equals the conjunction or disjunction,
according to $\mathit{isAnd}$, of the restricted evaluations of its children. That is,
$\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,\rho$ sends $x$
to the fold-$\mathrm{and}$ over $cs$ of $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho\,x$ when
$\mathit{isAnd}$ is true, and to the corresponding fold-$\mathrm{or}$ otherwise.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[AND of children has a small CNF]
\lean{LMN.and_children_have_cnf}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF, CNF.eval, CNF.width, LMN.compression_and_of_cnfs, LMN.restrictFn_node_eval, LMN.restricted_has_small_cnf_of_dtDepth_le, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If every child $c \in cs$ satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho) \le l$,
then there is a CNF $\psi$ of width at most $l$ that computes the restriction of the AND node:
$\mathrm{CNF.eval}\,\psi\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathrm{true}\,cs))\,\rho\,x$
for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[OR of children has a small DNF]
\lean{LMN.or_children_have_dnf}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, DNF, DNF.eval, DNF.width, LMN.compression_or_of_dnfs, LMN.restrictFn_node_eval, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.dtDepth_le_implies_small_dnf_cnf, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
If every child $c \in cs$ satisfies $\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho) \le l$,
then there is a DNF $\varphi$ of width at most $l$ that computes the restriction of the OR node:
$\mathrm{DNF.eval}\,\varphi\,x = \mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathrm{false}\,cs))\,\rho\,x$
for all $x$.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Compression and switching for a node]
\lean{LMN.compress_and_switch}
\leanok
\uses{BoolCircuit.Circuit, BoolCircuit.Circuit.eval, CNF.eval, DNF.eval, LMN.and_children_have_cnf, LMN.composeRestr, LMN.or_children_have_dnf, LMN.restrictFn_composeRestr, LMN.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_cnf_general, LMN.switching_bernoulli_dtDepth_dnf_general, SwitchingLemma2.Restriction, SwitchingLemma2.bernoulliRestrProb, SwitchingLemma2.restrictFn, dtDepth}
Suppose $0 < l$, $0 < n$, and every child $c \in cs$ satisfies
$\mathrm{dtDepth}(\mathrm{restrictFn}\,c.\mathrm{eval}\,\rho_1) \le l$. Then under a further
Bernoulli random restriction $\rho_2$ with parameter $1/(40 l)$, the probability that the
restricted node $\mathrm{restrictFn}\,(\mathrm{eval}\,(\mathrm{node}\,\mathit{isAnd}\,cs))\,(\mathrm{composeRestr}\,\rho_1\,\rho_2)$
has decision-tree depth greater than $t$ is at most
\[
  \left(\tfrac{1}{2}\right)^{t} + \exp\!\left(-\tfrac{n}{120 l}\right).
\]
\end{lemma}
